notices.title: Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
EAGLE MOUNTAIN CITY COUNCIL
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARINGS

The Eagle Mountain City Council will hold public hearings on May 5, 2026, at 7:00 PM or as soon thereafter as business allows, in the Eagle Mountain City Council Chambers, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, Utah 84005.  The City Council will receive public comments concerning the following items:

Parks, Trails and Open Space Master Plan and IFFP:

-	Eagle Mountain has contracted with MHTN to update the Parks, Trails and Open Space Plan. This effort includes a comprehensive inventory of the City's existing parks and recreational assets, an evaluation of current conditions, and an analysis of level of service to identify gaps and opportunities. The plan also outlines strategic recommendations to guide parks development and improvements over the next several years, including project prioritization based on community needs, service equity, and long-term buildout. An updated and associated Impact Fee Facilities Plan (IFFP) is also proposed. EMC Point of Contact: Assistant Public Works Director, Zac Hilton: zhilton@eaglemountian.gov

Brandon Park:

-	The Eagle Mountain City Council will conduct public hearings for proposed Future Land Use Map and Zoning Map amendments. These properties are located within the Brandon Park development. Approximately 216 acres of land are included in this proposal. The properties in question, along with the specific zoning and future land use designations proposed for each property, including a map, can be found by scanning the QR code provided below. EMC Point of Contact: Planner, Steven Lehmitz: slehmitz@eaglemountin.gov

Amendments to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code:

-	EMMC Chapter 17.72: Consideration of an amendment to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code Section 17.72 regarding drive-through locations and structured parking. EMC Point of Contact: Senior Planner, David Stroud: dstroud@eaglemountian.gov

-	EMMC Chapter 17.55: Consideration of an amendment to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code Section 17.55 regarding drive-through screening requirements, and the designated land use authority. EMC Point of Contact: Senior Planner, David Stroud: dstroud@eaglemountain.gov

Materials for public hearing items may be viewed within three days of the meeting on the city website and at the Eagle Mountain City Recorder's Office, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, UT, during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM. If you need special accommodation to participate in the meeting, please call the City Recorder's Office at 801.789.6611.  Open portions of the meeting can be viewed at www.eaglemountain.gov. 

THE PUBLIC IS INVITED TO PARTICIPATE IN ALL CITY COUNCIL MEETINGS.

notices.title: Notice of Quorum
notices.agenda_text:
EAGLE MOUNTAIN CITY
NOTICE OF POSSIBLE QUORUM

Notice is hereby given that a quorum of the Eagle Mountain City Council may be present at the Utah League of Cities and Towns Spring Conference to be held Wednesday, April 22, 2026, through Friday, April 24, 2026, at the Dixie Convention Center located at 1835 S. Convention Center Drive, St George, UT 84790. This is an educational and social training event with no actions or approvals being taken. 

CERTIFICATE OF POSTING
The undersigned, duly appointed City Recorder, does hereby certify that the above notice of quorum was posted on this 20th day of April, 2026, on the Eagle Mountain City bulletin boards, the Eagle Mountain City website www.eaglemountain.gov, and posted to the Utah State Public Notice website http://www.utah.gov/pmn/index.html
Lacie Messerly, City Recorder

notices.title: Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
EAGLE MOUNTAIN CITY 
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARINGS

The Eagle Mountain City Council will hold public hearings on Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 7:00 PM or as soon thereafter as business allows, in the Eagle Mountain City Council Chambers, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, Utah 84005.  The City Council will receive public comments concerning the following items:

Land Disposal Application - Chelsea Lindsey & Chad Vidinhar: 

In accordance with EMMC 3.30, the Eagle Mountain City Council may hold a public hearing to consider the Land Disposal Application from Chelsea Lindsey and Chad Vidinhar to dispose of approximately 0.10 acres (approximately 4,200 square feet) of real public property, identified as a portion of Utah County Parcel #46:536:0430 located in the Meadow Ranch Subdivision. The City has designated this parcel as 'Significant Real Property'.

EMC Staff Point of Contact: 
City Recorder Lacie A. Messerly lmesserly@eaglemountain.gov. 



All interested parties are invited to attend and provide comment. Written comments may also be submitted prior to the hearing to the City Recorder at recorder@eaglemountain.gov. or online using the Public Comment Form. Materials for public hearing items may be viewed within three days of the meeting on the City website and at the Eagle Mountain City Recorder's Office, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, UT, during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM. If you need special accommodation to participate in the meeting, please call the City Recorder's Office at 801.789.6611.  Open portions of the meeting can be viewed at www.eaglemountain.gov.

THE PUBLIC IS INVITED TO PARTICIPATE IN ALL CITY COUNCIL MEETINGS.

notices.title: Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
EAGLE MOUNTAIN CITY COUNCIL
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARINGS

The Eagle Mountain City Council will hold public hearings on April 21, 2026, at 7:00 PM or as soon thereafter as business allows, in the Eagle Mountain City Council Chambers, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, Utah 84005.  The City Council will receive public comments concerning the following items:

Amendments to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code:

-	EMMC Chapters 17.60 and 17.10 Fencing Amendment: Consideration of amendments to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code Sections 17.60.120 and 17.10.030 to add 'pre-weathered weathering steel' as an approved material type for residential perimeter fencing and to define 'pre-weathered weathering steel fences.' Initiated by Eagle Mountain Resident, Katie Elliott. This item was tabled by City Council for updates on 3/3/2026 EMC Point of Contact: Planner, Mickie Mills mmills@eaglemountain.gov. 

-	EMMC Chapter 17.72.040: Consideration of amendment to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code Section 17.72.040 to replace existing provisions with more specific standards for the screening of outdoor HVAC condenser units installed for multi-family buildings. EMC Point of Contact: Senior Planner, Robert Hobbs: rhobbs@eaglemountain.gov. 

Materials for public hearing items may be viewed within three days of the meeting on the city website and at the Eagle Mountain City Recorder's Office, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, UT, during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM. If you need special accommodation to participate in the meeting, please call the City Recorder's Office at 801.789.6611.  Open portions of the meeting can be viewed at www.eaglemountain.gov. 

THE PUBLIC IS INVITED TO PARTICIPATE IN ALL CITY COUNCIL MEETINGS.

notices.title: Public Meeting
notices.agenda_text:
EAGLE MOUNTAIN
CITY COUNCIL MEETING

APRIL 21, 2026, 4:00 PM
EAGLE MOUNTAIN CITY COUNCIL CHAMBERS
1650 E STAGECOACH RUN, EAGLE MOUNTAIN, UT 84005

4:00 PM WORK SESSION - CITY COUNCIL CHAMBERS


1.	DISCUSSION AND INFORMATION ITEMS
	This is an opportunity to provide information to the City Council. These items are for information only and do not require action by the City Council.

	1.A.	DISCUSSION - Quarterly Financial Report - Management Analyst Terrence Dela Peña
(anticipated time 15 minutes)

	1.B.	DISCUSSION - Trails at Hidden Valley Subdivision Concept Plan (formerly 'Lower Hidden Valley' Subdivision) - Community Development Director Brandon Larsen
(anticipated time 20 minutes)

	1.C.	DISCUSSION - Brandon Park Rezone - Planner Steven Lehmitz
(anticipated time 15 minutes)

2.	CITY MANAGER INFORMATION ITEMS
	This is an opportunity for the City Manager to provide information to the City Council. 

3.	AGENDA REVIEW
	The City Council will review items on the Consent Agenda and Policy Session Agenda.

4.	ADJOURN TO A CLOSED SESSION
	The City Council will adjourn into a Closed Session for the purpose of discussion of pending or reasonably imminent litigation; the character, professional competence, or physical or mental health of an individual; the purchase, lease, sale, or exchange of real property; a proposed development agreement, project proposal, or financing proposal related to the development of land owned by the state or a political subdivision; and/or the deployment of security personnel, devices, or systems, pursuant to Section 52-4-205(1) of the Utah Code, Annotated.

7:00 PM POLICY SESSION - CITY COUNCIL CHAMBERS

5.	CALL TO ORDER

6.	PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE

7.	INFORMATION ITEMS/UPCOMING EVENTS

	7.A.	RECOGNITION - Graduates of Eagle Mountain Academy Spring 2026

8.	PUBLIC COMMENTS
	Time has been set aside for the public to express their ideas, concerns, and comments. 
(Please limit your comments to three minutes each.)

9.	CITY COUNCIL/MAYOR ITEMS
	Time has been set aside for the City Council and Mayor to make comments.

CONSENT AGENDA

10.	CHANGE ORDERS

	10.A.	CHANGE ORDER - Belle Street Booster Power Conduit Replacement and SCADA - Change Orders #2 and #3

11.	MINUTES

	11.A.	April 7, 2026 Minutes - Regular City Council Meeting

	11.B.	April 14, 2026 Minutes - Budget Work Session

12.	RESOLUTIONS

	12.A.	RESOLUTION - A Resolution of Eagle Mountain City, Establishing Special Revenue Funds Titled the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Fund, Recreation, Arts, and Parks (RAP) Tax Fund and Community Development Fund, and Public Art Fund.

	12.B.	RESOLUTION - A Resolution of Eagle Mountain City, Utah, Declaring Surplus Property - Fleet Vehicles.

	12.C.	RESOLUTION - A Resolution of Eagle Mountain City, Utah, Awarding the Bid for the EMC Work Authorization GMP 1, Membrane Filtration System (MFS) Equipment Procurement with MGC Contractors, Inc. for the WWTF 6.0 MGD Expansion Project.

	12.D.	RESOLUTION - A Resolution of Eagle Mountain City, Utah, Approving the 2025 Municipal Wastewater Planning Program Annual Report.

SCHEDULED ITEMS

13.	RESOLUTIONS/PUBLIC HEARINGS

	13.A.	RESOLUTION/PUBLIC HEARING - A Resolution of Eagle Mountain City, Utah, Considering the Disposal of a Portion of Utah County Parcel #46:536:0430, Designated as 'Significant Real Property' Pursuant to EMMC 3.30.060 and 3.30.070.
BACKGROUND: (Presented by City Recorder Lacie A. Messerly) This item is a request to dispose of a portion of Utah County Parcel #46:536:0430 designated as 'Significant Real Property' in accordance with EMMC 3.30.060 and 3.30.070. The application, submitted by Chelsea Lindsay & Chad Vidinhar, pertains to a trail corridor located behind lot 424 in Meadow Ranch, Plat 4.

14.	ORDINANCES/PUBLIC HEARINGS

	14.A.	ORDINANCE/PUBLIC HEARING - An Ordinance of Eagle Mountain City, Utah, Amending the Eagle Mountain Municipal Code 17.72.040 HVAC Screening for Multi-Family Buildings.

BACKGROUND: (Presented by Senior Planner, Robert Hobbs) A proposed amendment to Eagle Mountain Code section 17.72.040 establishing more specific standards for the screening of outdoor HVAC condenser units installed for multi-family buildings. 

15.	ORDINANCES

	15.A.	ORDINANCE - An Ordinance of Eagle Mountain City, Utah, Amending the Eagle Mountain Municipal Code 17.60 and 17.10 regarding Fencing.

BACKGROUND: (Presented by Planner, Mickie Mills) Initiated by Eagle Mountain Resident, Katie Elliott, the proposed code amends Eagle Mountain Municipal Code 17.60.120 and 17.10.030 to add 'pre-weathered weathering steel (ASTM A606-4/A588 steel)' as an approved material type for residential perimeter fencing and defines 'pre-weathered weathering steel fences.' This item was tabled by City Council for updates on March 3, 2026. A public hearing has been held on this item.

16.	LEGISLATIVE ITEMS

	16.A.	UPDATE - Legislative Priorities List

17.	CITY COUNCIL/MAYOR'S BUSINESS AND LIAISON REPORTS
	This time is set aside for the City Council's and Mayor's comments on City business, and for Councilmembers to report on the boards they are assigned to as liaisons to the City Council. 

18.	COMMUNICATION ITEMS

	18.A.	Financial Report

	18.B.	Upcoming Agenda Items

19.	ADJOURNMENT
 
THE PUBLIC IS INVITED TO PARTICIPATE IN PUBLIC MEETINGS FOR ALL AGENDAS.
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, Eagle Mountain City will make reasonable accommodations for participation in all Public Meetings and Work Sessions. Please call the City Recorder's Office at least 3 working days prior to the meeting at 801-789-6611. This meeting may be held telephonically to allow a member of the public body to participate. This agenda is subject to change with a minimum 24-hour notice.

CERTIFICATE OF POSTING

The undersigned, duly appointed City Recorder, does hereby certify that the above agenda notice was posted on this 16 day of April, 2026, on the Eagle Mountain City bulletin boards, the Eagle Mountain City website www.eaglemountain.gov, posted to the Utah State public notice website http://www.utah.gov/pmn/index.html.

Lacie A. Messerly
City Recorder

notices.title: Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
PUBLIC HEARING NOTICE
Community Development Block Grant (CDBG)
Eagle Mountain City will hold a public hearing on Tuesday April 7th at 7:00 p.m. at 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain City, Utah 84005 to discuss the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program year July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. The amount of CDBG funds for this program year will be discussed along with the range of projects eligible.
The CDBG program is a federally funded program administered by the State of Utah, Department of Workforce Services, Housing and Community Development Division, and provides funding for eligible projects that benefit low- and moderate-income (LMI) persons.
The purpose of this hearing is to provide information about the CDBG program and to receive public input regarding community development needs and potential projects that may be considered for funding. Suggestions will be solicited verbally, electronically, and in writing from all interested parties during the public comment period from March 16 - April 15, 2026. Eligible activities may include public infrastructure improvements, community facilities, housing-related activities, economic development projects, and certain public services.
To comment or for further information, contact Taylor Draney at 801-547-7594 or tdraney@magutah.gov
If Eagle Mountain City chooses to submit a CDBG application, a second public hearing will be held at a later date to review the proposed project.
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, individuals needing special accommodations should contact Taylor Draney at least five (5) days prior to the hearing. Individuals with hearing or speech impairments may dial 711 (Relay Utah).
Materials for public hearing items may be viewed within three days of the meeting on the city website and at the Eagle Mountain City Recorder's Office, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, UT, during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM. If you need special accommodation to participate in the meeting, please call the City Recorder's Office at 801.789.6610. Open portions of the meeting can be viewed at www.eaglemountain.gov.

THE PUBLIC IS INVITED TO PARTICIPATE IN ALL CITY COUNCIL MEETINGS.
